method: 4-persona debate (theorist/engineer/skeptic/pm) over literature_pool.json + openreview_extracts; full persona notes in persona_notes/

clusters.underlying_issue: Latent/continuous multimodal reasoning lacks (a) principled objectives for what a latent thought must encode, (b) adaptive computation — every method uses fixed latent length/uniform budget, and (c) confound-controlled evaluation separating 'thinking in latent space' from 'extra fine-tuning compute'.
personas: T1 (ad-hoc latent targets; AMVL posterior-shortcut evidence)
T3 (no stopping rule; DLWM uniform-budget complaint)
S1 (LVR reviews: single dataset, stage-2 text-FT drives gains, baseline access unclear)
E1 (efficiency claimed, wall-clock rarely reported; ThinkMorph interleaved generation cost)
P1 (decode-side tokens are the real latency/energy cost per arxiv:2607.09520)
reviewer_quotes: The reliance on a fixed latent reasoning length may restrict flexibility and generalization to variable reasoning complexity. (LVR review)
This concern is reinforced by Table 4, which shows that the first training stage (latent grounding) has substantially less impact than the second (text-only fine-tuning). (LVR review)
However, generating N interleaved chains, each containing images, is computationally expensive. (ThinkMorph review)

clusters.underlying_issue: Internal hallucination predictors in LVLMs (hidden-state similarity, attention degradation, key-norms, SAE features, language-prior dominance) are used as if causal but validated only correlationally; steering/decoding fixes built on them have unmeasured side effects (refusal rate up, OOD accuracy down) and unquantified latency/memory overhead.
personas: T2 (Token-Guard: cosine-similarity-factuality link unverified; HalluGuard: 'extremely loose' theory)
S3 (QA-only benchmarks; CASAL OOD refusal/accuracy side effects)
reviewer_quotes: A key concern is that the paper does not clearly establish the causal connection between SAE features and hallucination behavior. (Snowballing review)

The definition of latent hallucination scoring is intuitive but not empirically verified; it is unclear whether cosine similarity between hidden states best correlates with factual accuracy. (Token-Guard review)
It seems the refusal rate increased and accuracy dropped after CASAL in OOD generalization. (CASAL review)
The framework assumes that hallucination arises primarily from attention degradation, but other causes of hallucination are not addressed. (Snowballing review)

clusters.underlying_issue: RL for multimodal reasoning (GRPO-family) reports aggregate gains without attributing them between perception errors and reasoning errors; reviewers repeatedly ask 'what fraction of baseline failures are perception failures and which does RL fix?', and text-heavy RL may silently degrade visual grounding (catastrophic forgetting unmeasured).
personas: S4 (gains vs plain GRPO marginal; single backbone family)
E4 (rollout compute makes reproduction hard; encourages under-ablated papers)
reviewer_quotes: What percentage of the baseline's failures on these tasks are due to perception errors, and what percentage of those specific errors does Perception-R1 fix? (Perception-R1 review)

The reviewer notes that GRPO is also used in Vision-R1, making it unclear how much of the improvement is attributable to the novel contribution versus the baseline RL algorithm. (Perception-R1 review)
After text-only cold-start SFT and text-only RL, visual related ability could degrade. Did you measure vision-only tasks before & after? (Revisual-R1 review)

clusters.underlying_issue: Visual-token importance metrics are unreliable (random pruning matches sophisticated metrics; key-norm heuristics unvalidated), and the entire visual-token-reduction agenda may target the wrong bottleneck — decode-side generation dominates edge VLM energy/latency, not vision encoding.
personas: S3b (matched-budget comparisons missing across pruning papers)
P1 (arxiv:2607.09520: 'Seeing is Free, Speaking is Not')
reviewer_quotes: The key-norm heuristic used as a substitute for attention may not perfectly capture true token importance, and the paper does not analyze failure cases. (Snowballing review)
clusters.note: Folded partially into C1 (decode-side cost motivates latent/short reasoning) and C2 (unvalidated importance signals).
